other_observers: Norm Erthal, Glenn Walbek, Ric Olson, Paul Differding, Loch Kilpatrick; I was the original finder of the bird, however Norm, Ric and Paul were present.  Glenn and Loch came later and we helped them relocate the bird.
specific_location: Chatfield State Park; Marina sandspit by the southeast marina
description: Small flycatcher with dark eyes and a thin, black bill. Crown, auriculars, nape, wings and tail all a uniform medium brown color. Back was gray-brown. Thin white supercillium noted from the beak to behind the eye. Faint black eye-stripe present. Chin and throat white; breast white with faint brown streaks. Belly and flanks salmon colored. Wings brown with two faint white wingbars. Legs black.Behaviors: Flycatching for insects from small cottonwoods and from the shoreline.Call: No vocalizations were heardPlumage: adult female
photographer: Photographer: Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner
